description_long: This is a classic lever-action rifle featuring a wooden stock and forearm with a rich, reddish-brown finish, paired with a long, blued steel barrel and receiver. The lever loop is prominent beneath the receiver, allowing for manual cycling of the action. The rifle has a tubular magazine running parallel beneath the barrel, typical of traditional lever-action designs. Iron sights are mounted on the barrel, with a simple front post and a rear notch. The overall design is streamlined and functional, with minimal embellishments, evoking a utilitarian yet elegant appearance. The materials and construction suggest durability and reliability, suitable for both display and practical use.
description: A classic lever-action rifle with a wooden stock and long blued steel barrel, featuring iron sights and a tubular magazine. The design is streamlined and functional, with a traditional appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the wooden stock, lever, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Front view down the barrel, emphasizing the narrow profile and sight alignment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the lever, wooden stock, and tubular magazine beneath the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view from the buttstock, showing the width of the stock and the alignment of the barrel.
